title: Akash Vishwanath More of Congress to contest Baramati Bypoll

title: Development, welfare, and civic problems shape the poll mood in Thiru. Vi. Ka. Nagar

title: Davangere South: Close contest on the cards while minorities are divided over supporting Shamanur family
content: As the campaigning for the bypoll is nearing its conclusion, the decision of the minorities who are divided over supporting Shamanur family, will decide whether the family’s sway over Davangere continues.

title: Transforming India’s nuclear power landscape
content: Realising the 100 GW target requires SHANTI Act implementation alongside transparent resolution of tariffs, fuel ownership, waste management, insurance, dispute settlement, and regulatory autonomy

title: Tamil Nadu missed out on Central government schemes because DMK saw BJP as an enemy, says G.K. Vasan
content: This is an election for the safety of the people of Tamil Nadu, especially women. The DMK does not want any development from the Centre because of vote bank politics. It should be a double-engine sarkar; the Central and State governments should work together, says the TMC(M) president

title: Wayanad relief fund issue sets political tone in last lap of electioneering in Kerala Assembly polls
content: The Congress has found itself on the defensive, as the CPI (M) has sought the details of the funds collected by the party and its utilisation

title: When an unknown 29-year-old took on K. Kamaraj in 1962
content: Sixty-four years ago, P. Ramamoorthy, now 92, volunteered to fight against the then all-powerful Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu in Sattur, though he had nothing personal against him. The election did not generate any surprise. Kamaraj won hands down

title: Sabala food festival draws 50,000 visitors in Visakhapatnam
content: Focus on natural farming, traditional diets and sustainable living attracts large crowds over five days

title: A Congress veteran calls for making the party a people’s movement
content: Dhanuskodi Athithan, a long-standing Congress leader and former Union Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sport, wants the party to do field work and take up people’s issues. “Look at Tirunelveli and Kanniyakumari districts where the party is still strong. This is because of this approach,” he points out

title: I do not see the Assembly election in Tamil Nadu as a close contest, says CM Stalin
content: The contest is essentially between the DMK-led front and the AIADMK-led alliance. Our achievements and electoral promises have captured the imagination of the people. The outcome will disprove the notion that the DMK cannot win two consecutive elections, says the DMK president

title: Toothless ban: Single-use plastic rules 84% of surveyed sites in four cities
content: A New Delhi-based environmental research and advocacy organisation studied 560 locations in Bhubaneswar, Delhi, Guwahati, and Mumbai

title: A debate on the tenure of Election Commission-appointed police chief
content: However, the EC order did not specify a tenure for him. The question is whether Mr. Rathore would be entitled to a minimum two-year tenure, as mandated by the Supreme Court in the  landmark Prakash Singh case. However, this ruling applies only to appointments made through the UPSC-led process

title: Tejashwi challenges Bihar CM, deputy CMs for a public debate over State’s progress
content: The RJD leader said that under the NDA government of 21 years and the double-engine government of 12 years, Bihar has been at the bottom rung in most social, human and economic indicators

title: Ganga water sharing, visas, energy cooperation likely on agenda for Bangladesh FM visit
content: Visit likely on April 7 and 8; first FM visit since fall of Hasina regime; newly elected BNP indicates more willingness to negotiate Ganga water sharing treaty renewal, compared to the interim government

title: Rajamahendravaram set to host international water sport event, to begin from May 30
content: International Dragon Boat League 2026 to attract talent from 25 countries, of which 12 countries have confirmed their participation in the three-day event; the event is part of a larger vision to make the city a global hub for water sports

title: Christians in Kerala celebrate Easter with prayers, festivities
content: Special prayers and services were conducted in churches of various denominations, with bishops and senior clergy leading the liturgy

title: The executive office without a limit
content: India has developed a convention limiting a third presidential term, but the Constitution places no such restriction on the Prime Minister’s tenure. With Prime Minister Narendra Modi completing 8,931 days in office, this asymmetry invites closer scrutiny.

title: Price crash: Anxiety over slow liquidation of tobacco on auction platforms
content: Although the auction season commences in September–October and concludes by March every year, over 24 million kg of tobacco have remain unsold

title: Why vocal about infiltration only in Assam: Manipur-based Naga group asks PM

title: Weapon of mass distraction: Congress slams Govt.’s delimitation proposal

Siddaramaiah says five southern States together gain barely 63-66 additional seats, while just seven BJP-dominated States gain about 128-131 seats – nearly double

title: ED to investigate into assets of self-styled godman Kharat; call record leaks will be probed: Fadnavis

content: Arvind Kejriwal, along with several other accused, has moved an application seeking the recusal of Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from hearing the matter.

title: Odisha Man Rapes Specially-Abled Daughter. She Gives Birth To Baby Girl
content: A man has been arrested for allegedly raping his differently-abled daughter repeatedly in Odisha's Dhenkanal district after she gave birth to a baby girl.

title: Russian Missile Designer Alexander Leonov Who Developed Brahmos Dies At 74
content: Leonov was CEO and Chief Designer of NPO Mashinostroyenia (NPOMASH), the Russian JV partner in New Delhi-based BrahMos Aerospace.

title: US Almost Did Not Rescue Its F-15 Airman Thinking It Was An Iranian Trap
content: The F-15E was shot down over southwestern Iran on Friday, the first confirmed loss of a US combat aircraft inside Iran since the war began.

content: The threats followed the extraction of a US Air Force weapons systems officer whose F-15E Strike Eagle aircraft was downed inside Iran

title: Delhi Bans LPG Cylinder Sales From Godowns, 17 Raids Conducted Across City
content: Delhi has barred the sale of LPG cylinders directly from godowns, with authorities warning of strict action as enforcement intensifies across the city.

content: With less than three weeks to go for the election, campaigning in West Bengal has entered an intense phase, with top political leaders fanning out across the state to galvanise voters.

title: Deadline For Iran To Open Strait of Hormuz Is Tuesday Evening: Trump
content: US President Donald Trump said his deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz or face attacks on critical infrastructure is Tuesday evening, according to an interview he gave to the Wall Street Journal published on Sunday.

title: MS Dhoni Gets Housing Board Notice Over 'Commercial Use' Of Ranchi Home
content: Former Indian cricket team captain Mahendra Singh Dhoni has been served a notice by the Jharkhand State Housing Board over alleged commercial use of his residential plot in Ranchi.

title: "Whole Region Going To Burn": Iranian Parliament Speaker's Warning To Trump
content: Iran's powerful parliament speaker warned US President Donald Trump on Sunday that his "reckless moves" would mean "our whole region is going to burn".

content: On April 3, an F-15E Strike Eagle, a twin-engine, two-seat interdiction fighter jet, was downed over southwestern Iran.

title: After Trump's "Crazy Bast*rds" Post, Iran Sends 'Remember This' Message
content: "History repeats itself. Operation Eagle Claw, a historic US military failure in Iran's Tabas Desert. April 24, 1980," Iran's embassy said in a post on X

title: Himanta Sarma Responds After Congress Claims His Wife Holds 3 Passports
content: After Congress claims on his wife, Himanta Sarma pointed out six anomalies that included mismatch in name, UAE ID, and nationality

title: Air India Stops Flights To Israel Till May 31 Amid Iran War
content: Most of the leading airlines have suspended their operations on the Tel Aviv route with only Israeli carriers like El Al, IsraAir, Arkia and Air Haifa operating under severe restrictions.

title: Trump Says "Good Chance" Of Deal With Iran On Monday
content: US President Donald Trump said Sunday he believes there is a "good chance" of making a deal with Iran on Monday, ahead of his deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face heavy bombing.

title: EAM Jaishankar receives call from Iran FM Araghchi as Trump’s 48-hour threat looms
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar spoke with his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi amid escalating Middle East tensions. The call, confirmed by the Iran Embassy in India, covered bilateral relations and regional developments. This follows Jaishankar's recent discussions with UAE and Qatar leaders, as Iran faces a US deadline over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Sabarimala case: SC sets up 9-judge bench on women’s entry to religious places
content: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench, comprising judges from diverse faiths and a woman, will hear crucial cases on women's rights versus religious practices starting April 7. This bench aims to settle long-standing disputes, including women's entry into temples, mosques, and Parsi fire temples, and the practice of FGM. The court's 2018 Sabarimala judgment sparked these broader legal challenges.

title: IPL 2026: Hat-trick of losses for CSK as RCB run riot in Bengaluru
content: Defending champions RCB extended their winning streak with a 43-run victory over CSK at Chinnaswamy Stadium. After a cautious start, Phil Salt (46) and Devdutt Padikkal (50) built momentum, while Tim David (70*) and Rajat Patidar (48*) unleashed a brutal assault to reach 250/3. CSK’s chase faltered early despite Sarfaraz Khan’s 50, and they were bowled out for 207.

title: Google, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta face an American data centre problem; here’s why
content: Tech giants like Google and Meta face mounting opposition across the US over massive data center projects. At least 11 states are proposing laws to restrict or pause new construction, driven by concerns over power grid strain, rising energy costs, and water usage.

title: Easter Sunday, Bloody Monday: Trump's unholy hellfire warning to Iran
content: US President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran, threatening severe retaliation if the Strait of Hormuz remained closed. His unvarnished social media posts, including profanity, signaled potential strikes on Iranian infrastructure like power plants and bridges. This aggressive stance has ignited debate over humanitarian impact and international law, with critics fearing civilian harm and supporters citing military necessity.

title: BJP office blast: ISI plot hatched by handlers in Portugal & Germany, says Punjab DGP
content: Seven suspects have been arrested in connection with the grenade blast outside the Punjab BJP HQ in Chandigarh, orchestrated by ISI-linked plotters in Europe for Rs 2 lakh. The accused were directed by foreign-based handlers Baljot Singh and Harjeet Singh, with two main perpetrators lobbing the grenade after receiving arms and explosives.

title: Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing about AI layoffs
content: Kevin O'Leary, the Canadian billionaire, asserts that artificial intelligence is elevating human creativity, making artists and storytellers more valuable than ever. He highlights how compelling social media content, especially short-form videos, now commands significant salaries due to measurable customer acquisition. O'Leary believes AI is a powerful productivity tool, fostering new industries and opportunities across all economic sectors.

title: RCB vs CSK: Tim David goes ballistic, equals AB de Villiers record to ...
content: Batting in the lower middle order at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium, David struck eight sixes during his innings. That effort is now the joint second-highest number of sixes hit by an RCB batter from No.5 or lower in an IPL match.

title: Meta CTO’s message to engineers and college graduates looking to join technology industry
content: Meta's CTO, Andrew Bosworth, urged aspiring tech professionals to prioritize hands-on building over theoretical study. He emphasized immersing oneself in practical projects, from "vibe coding" with AI tools for software to using Raspberry Pi and Arduino for hardware.  Bosworth advocates for continuous experimentation and action to develop essential skills for the evolving tech landscape.

title: Kohli's no-look six leaves Anushka impressed; her reaction goes viral - Watch
content: Kohli was batting after RCB were put in. He got an early lifeline when Shivam Dube dropped a catch. Soon after, Kohli hit Henry for a six when the ball was pitched in his hitting range, swinging across the line to clear the boundary.

title: Candace Owens claims win as Blake Lively case vs Justin Baldoni dismissed
content: Candace Owens reacted after a federal judge dismissed most of Blake Lively’s claims against Justin Baldoni, leaving only three issues for trial. The ruling removed serious allegations like sexual harassment and defamation. The case will now focus on retaliation claims when the trial begins on May 18, 2026, as reported by The Guardian and Entertainment Weekly.

title: Taylor Swift may change wedding plans amid pressure before Travis Kelce big day
content: Taylor Swift is reportedly facing mounting stress while planning her wedding with Travis Kelce, as tight scheduling, public leaks, and complex logistics take a toll. At one point, she even considered changing plans to maintain privacy. Despite sleepless nights and pressure, the couple remains focused on creating an intimate, meaningful ceremony while balancing Kelce’s NFL commitments and Swift’s vision.

title: Packers linked to $6M ex-Commanders veteran after strong season performance
content: The Green Bay Packers recorded a pretty average performance in the last season with 9 wins and 8 losses, sparking stress among fans. While the team’s performance declined since the 2024 season, when they had recorded 11 wins and just 6 losses, it seems the Green Bay Packers are fully focused on fixing their roster and could be eyeing a veteran.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for April 6
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 6, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: The missing contest: Why 2026 elections are not about PM Modi and Rahul Gandhi
content: Far from being a PM Modi vs Rahul showdown, the 2026 elections are a test of regional resilience. Despite the visibility of the two national leaders on the campaign trail, neither is the decisive factor in these contests. Instead, the real battles are being fought by state satraps defending their turf or reclaiming relevance and the results will hinge on regional leadership, local alliances and governance records.

title: 'Step forward': Sanjiv Goenka reacts after LSG survive last-over scare
content: Lucknow won by five wickets, chasing 157 with one ball to spare. Captain Rishabh Pant guided the chase after Hyderabad had put up a fight.

title: A threat to commit war crimes? Why Trump's new warning to Iran has shocked world
content: President Trump's inflammatory social media post threatening Iran with attacks on power plants and bridges has sparked grave concerns among international law experts. They warn such actions could constitute war crimes, violating principles against targeting civilian infrastructure and causing disproportionate harm to the populace. This rhetoric risks undermining established rules of engagement.

title: Tim David, Patidar fire 97-run barrage in death overs; RCB reach highest IPL 2026 total
content: The late surge came from Tim David, captain Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, who built on the base set earlier in the innings. Padikkal scored 50 off 29 balls, David made 70 off 25 balls, while Patidar remained unbeaten on 48 off 19 balls.

title: ‘Challenging time’: India sends aid to earthquake & flood-hit Afghanistan
content: India has dispatched humanitarian assistance and disaster relief materials to Afghanistan following recent devastating floods and earthquakes. The aid includes essential items like kitchen sets, hygiene kits, and shelter materials. This gesture underscores India's solidarity with the Afghan people during this challenging period of overlapping natural disasters.

title: 'US sent guns to Iran protesters': Trump's big revelation on why weapons never reached
content: US President Donald Trump claimed America sent weapons to Iranian protesters through Kurds but the group kept them. He also warned Iran of severe consequences if a deal is not made soon. Trump threatened to destroy power plants and infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not opened. He previously set similar deadlines which were extended.

title: 12-year-old boy exposes mother, sister's plot to kill father in Uttarakhand
content: A woman and her daughter have been arrested for allegedly murdering the husband and father in Haridwar. The 12-year-old son's testimony exposed the crime, revealing the mother and daughter allegedly conspired to kill the victim due to his opposition to their separate relationships. The post-mortem confirmed strangulation.

title: IPL: Virat Kohli scripts history, breaks Rohit Sharma's record to ...
content: Playing at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium, the Royal Challengers Bengaluru opener went past Rohit Sharma to take his total against CSK to 1,174 runs. Kohli needed just two runs at the start of the match to break Rohit’s record of 1,161 runs against Kolkata Knight Riders, and reached the mark in the first over.

title: 'We got him': Trump claims victory as US aircraft wreckage smolders in desert
content: US forces executed a high-risk rescue of a downed airman deep in Iran, recovering him safely despite losing two C-130s and two Black Hawks. President Trump hailed the mission as a success, emphasizing the airman's recovery. Iran, however, claims to have shot down multiple US aircraft, portraying the operation as a defeat.

title: 'Will blow everything up and take the oil': Trump if Iran doesn't make a deal
content: US President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran, threatening to "blow everything up and take over the oil" if a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz isn't reached swiftly. Tensions escalated following US-Israeli strikes on Iran, disrupting oil markets and regional stability.

title: 'Trying to import worst elements': Mamata targets BJP ahead of polls, urges vigil on EVMs
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee accused the BJP of bringing in "paid supporters" and hooliganism to influence elections, while also warning her party workers about potential EVM tampering. Union Home Minister Amit Shah urged voters to cast ballots without fear, as Prime Minister Modi criticized the state's law and order.

title: LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka's reaction goes viral as team registers first win
content: After opting to bowl first at the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium, Lucknow Super Giants got early wickets. Shami (2/9) struck early blows, dismissing the opening duo of Abhishek Sharma (0) and Travis Head (7) in successive overs. Prince Yadav then removed SRH captain Ishan Kishan, leaving the home side under pressure early in the innings.

title: IPL: Pant’s mature 68 powers LSG to five-wicket win over SRH
content: Mohammed Shami's exceptional opening spell choked Sunrisers Hyderabad, but a strong recovery led by Klaasen and Reddy set a competitive target. Rishabh Pant anchored Lucknow Super Giants' chase, guiding them to a thrilling five-wicket victory with a composed unbeaten half-century in the final over.

title: 'Goodwill’ lift turned fatal: Engineering students kill drunk driver, flee with car
content: Three engineering students, driven by greed for a red Alto car, murdered an intoxicated man they offered to help. After initially assisting Suchit Bhojapure, they decided to steal his vehicle and possessions, leading to his brutal killing and disposal of his body. The students were apprehended after blood stains on their shoes betrayed their lies.

title: National Maritime Day: India's oil lifeline churns through conflict waters
content: On most days, India's economic heartbeat is invisible. It does not pulse through stock exchanges or policy corridors but across vast, restless waters where ships move quietly between continents. Yet, when conflict erupts in narrow choke points like the Strait of Hormuz, that invisible system becomes the country’s most visible vulnerability. The ongoing tensions have done exactly that, forcing India to confront a reality it has long managed but rarely foregrounded.

title: MS Dhoni not playing vs RCB: When could he return for CSK?
content: MS Dhoni will miss CSK’s IPL 2026 clash against RCB due to a calf strain, with the team struggling after two losses. While RCB enter confidently, CSK feel his absence. The franchise said he’ll miss two weeks, though coach Stephen Fleming stressed his strong off-field influence. Dhoni could return against KKR on April 14, or later fixtures if recovery takes longer.

title: Rakesh Bedi reveals Sachin Tendulkar's reaction for his 'Dhurandhar 2' performance
content: Actor Rakesh Bedi is receiving widespread acclaim for his role as politician Jameel Jamali in 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'.  His performance, particularly a viral dialogue and a surprising confession of being an Indian spy, has resonated with audiences, even earning a call from Sachin Tendulkar.

title: ‘Drugged, raped, forced into marriage': Hyderabad woman alleges coerced conversion
content: she provided an outline of a harrowing experience involving deception, physical violence, financial exploitation, and religious coercion. The victim met the accused, Nawaz, when he approached her to rent a shop. She alleged he falsely claimed to have converted to Hinduism and changed his name to Navadurga to gain her trust.

title: Suspected ISIS-linked terror module busted, youth radicalisation plot uncovered
content: Authorities have busted a terror network allegedly linked to ISIS and foreign handlers, accused of radicalizing Indian youth online. Teams across multiple states apprehended suspects involved in spreading jihadi propaganda, including videos of extremist figures. The group reportedly aimed to establish an Islamic State in India, with links traced to Pakistan, Afghanistan, Syria, and Bangladesh.
